commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review should hold this for contributor-supplied service-env proof and maintainer acceptance of the compatibility/auth behavior; there is no narrow ClawSweeper code repair to queue.; Cleared: The diff changes local config parsing and tests only; I found no new dependency, workflow, package, secret exposure, or code-execution surface. (sha=d0fb8531e5ee9e137033ccbfd7341d8013347984)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds state-dir `.env` parsing logic that omits whole-value shell references before they are passed into managed service environments, plus focused Vitest coverage.

PR surface: Source +17, Tests +67. Total +84 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main passes parsed state-dir `.env` values into durable service env planning, and the service writers persist those values literally. I did not run a live gateway install in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should hold this for contributor-supplied service-env proof and maintainer acceptance of the compatibility/auth behavior; there is no narrow ClawSweeper code repair to queue.

target.title: fix(config): skip state-dir dotenv values that are unresolved shell references
target.body:
Fixes #88274

## Summary

`readStateDirDotEnvVarsFromStateDir` accepted every non-empty value from `~/.openclaw/.env` without checking whether the value is an unresolved shell variable reference. `dotenv.parse` preserves these references literally — it does not expand `$VAR` or `${VAR}`. When a literal reference like `${SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_KEY}` reaches the LaunchAgent/systemd wrapper, it is single-quoted and written to the service env file as-is, so the plugin receives the literal string `${SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_KEY}` instead of the actual credential value.

The fix adds `isUnresolvedShellReference()` and skips values whose **entire content** is a recognised shell reference form:

- `$VAR_NAME` — simple variable reference (must start with a letter or underscore)
- `${VAR_NAME}` — brace-form variable reference
- `$(command)` — command substitution

Real credentials that merely **contain** a `$` (e.g. a password `abc$2!xyz`, a price `$100`, or a token `tok_$prod_v2`) are **not** skipped — the regex is anchored to the whole value and requires the variable name to start with a letter or underscore.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `parseStateDirDotEnvContent` passes literal shell references like `${SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_KEY}` into the service env. After the fix, only values whose entire content is a recognised shell reference are skipped. Real credentials containing `$` in the middle or at the start of a dollar-number remain in the service env.

**Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node.js v22.22.0, HEAD `d0fb8531e5ee9e137033ccbfd7341d8013347984` based on `origin/main` `6399b6a4455d0a5373329dc8c71ff4b1da6f50c9`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts \
  src/config/state-dir-dotenv.test.ts \
  --reporter=verbose --testTimeout=10000
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```
$ OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts \
  src/config/state-dir-dotenv.test.ts \
  --reporter=verbose --testTimeout=10000

 RUN  v4.1.7 /media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw-issue-88274

 ✓ |runtime-config| src/config/state-dir-dotenv.test.ts > readStateDirDotEnvVarsFromStateDir > returns real credential values from the state-dir dotenv 7ms
 ✓ |runtime-config| src/config/state-dir-dotenv.test.ts > readStateDirDotEnvVarsFromStateDir > skips values that are unresolved shell variable references 3ms
 ✓ |runtime-config| src/config/state-dir-dotenv.test.ts > readStateDirDotEnvVarsFromStateDir > preserves credential values that merely contain a dollar sign 2ms
 ✓ |runtime-config| src/config/state-dir-dotenv.test.ts > readStateDirDotEnvVarsFromStateDir > returns empty object when .env is missing 1ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)
   Start at  03:04:40
   Duration  426ms (transform 80ms, setup 44ms, import 138ms, tests 17ms, environment 0ms)
```

The test `skips values that are unresolved shell variable references` runs `readStateDirDotEnvVarsFromStateDir` against a real temp dir `.env` with `SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_API_KEY="${SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_KEY}"` and verifies the reference is omitted, while `preserves credential values that merely contain a dollar sign` verifies that values like `abc$2!xyz` and `$100.price` are still passed through.

**Observed result after fix:** `parseStateDirDotEnvContent` skips `$VAR` and `${VAR}` whole-value references. Real credentials that merely contain a `$` character are preserved. The service env no longer receives literal shell reference strings for affected values.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end gateway service installation and restart on macOS. The filtering logic is exercised by regression tests using real dotenv parsing and real temp directories.
target.author: alix-007

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
